reasoning:
The knowledge cutoff is June 6, 2025, and the resolution date is July 1, 2025, leaving a window of only 25 days for the contract to be cancelled. Recent parliamentary hearings in May 2025 emphasized that the contract contains no standard termination clause, and a unilateral cancellation by Bulgaria would trigger a massive structural penalty of BGN 4.2 billion. Consequently, Bulgaria has been trying to renegotiate the contract rather than cancel it outright. 

On the other hand, Turkey has an enormously lucrative contract and has entirely rejected previous proposals to cancel or alter it. While Bulgargaz is heavily in arrears (approx. BGN 300 million as of May 2025) and has suspended some payments, BOTAŞ is disincentivized from cancelling the contract itself, as doing so might jeopardize its claims to future guaranteed payments beneath the take-or-pay agreement; it would far more likely pursue international arbitration. Because of the massive financial penalties preventing Bulgaria from cancelling and Turkey wanting to keep its advantageous deal intact, there is a very low chance of cancellation in the extremely short remaining 25-day timeframe.
